page: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU operations, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read bandwidth for small blocks and strong write performance for large blocks. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512 and AES instructions, yielding top-tier results in Passmark CPU extended instructions and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.12xlarge offers high resource density by pairing a large memory footprint with dedicated, high-frequency physical cores. This balance makes it highly effective for memory-intensive workloads without sacrificing raw compute throughput. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, Redis in-memory caching, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage. Benchmark results place the r8a.12xlarge in the top tier for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also demonstrates strong performance in cryptographic tests and medium-model LLM inference. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads requiring dedicated compute resources, making it an excellent fit for enterprise database management, large-scale Redis deployments, software compilation, and data-intensive processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 22 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier database and compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz.
Equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz.
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor.
Achieves top-tier benchmark results in database operations and Redis throughput.
Optimized for memory-intensive workloads, software compilation, and LLM inference.
